km.Stirlingshire, Scotland: ស្ទីរលីnហ្គស៊ីរe, ស្កុតឡេន, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston是Sir Alexander de Houston的兒子，於1239年出生。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1296年在英國蘇格蘭斯林史爾享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston是Sir Alexander de Houston的儿子，于1239年出生。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1296年在英国苏格兰斯林史爾享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Houston (Renfrewshire), from the genitive case of the personal name Hugh + Middle English, Older Scots ton ‘town, village, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’). The landlord in question was a certain Hugo de Paduinan, who held the place c. 1160. The Scottish surname is also common in Ulster.
Irish: variant of McCutcheon from Mac Uisdein or Mac Uistein.

History: In 1836 the newly founded city of Houston, TX, was named in honor of Sam Houston (1793–1863), soldier and statesman. As commander in chief of the Texan army he achieved Texas independence from Mexico by routing the army of the Mexican general Santa Anna. His ancestors were Ulster Scots who had emigrated to Philadelphia, PA, in the 18th century.

variants.name: Hueston
meanings: English (Middlesex) and Irish: variant of   Houston  .

variants.name: Heuston
meanings: Irish: variant of   Houston   or   Hewson  .

variants.name: Hoston
meanings: English: probably a variant of northern Irish   Houston  .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

variants.name: Hughston
meanings: Scottish: apparently a habitational name from somewhere in Britain, most probably a variant of Houston . There is also a small place called Hughston in the Highland region of Scotland.

variants.name: Huston
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Derry and Antrim): variant of   Houston  .

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston was born in 1239 as the son of Sir Alexander de Houston. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1296, in Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston est né en 1239, fils de Sir Alexander de Houston. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1296, à Stirlingshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston wurde 1239, als Sohn von Sir Alexander de Houston geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1296 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Stirlingshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston è nato il nel 1239 come figlio di Sir Alexander de Houston. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1296, nel Stirlingshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.Stirlingshire, Scotland: イギリススコットランドスティルリングシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houstonは1239年にSir Alexander de Houstonの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1296年にイギリススコットランドスティルリングシュアイアで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston(은)는 1239년 Sir Alexander de Houston의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1296년 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston nasceu em 1239 filho de Sir Alexander de Houston. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1296, em Stirlingshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston родился в 1239 г. Его отца звали Sir Alexander de Houston. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1296 г. в местности стирлингшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Simon de Houston nació en 1239 como hijo de Sir Alexander de Houston. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1296, en Stirlingshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 57 años.
